957.code: for (int i = 0; look(i).getType() != LexType.SEMICN && look(i) != null; i += 1) {
957.reason: [Potential NullPointerException: look(i) may return null, but the condition look(i) != null is checked after accessing look(i).getType(), causing a NullPointerException when look(i) is null.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Potential NullPointerException: look(i) may return null, but the condition look(i) != null is checked after accessing look(i).getType(), causing a NullPointerException when look(i) is null.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks `look(i).getType()` before checking for null, which can cause a NullPointerException when `look(i)` returns null. Also, the loop may never terminate if a semicolon is missing, leading to an index out-of-bounds error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  look(i).getType() is evaluated before the null check, causing a NullPointerException when look(i) returns null. The null check must come first.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: look(i) may be null, calling .getType() before the null check causes a NullPointerException]
